text.text:
Good - all layout references use either `layouts/base` or `layouts/main`. Now let me do one final comprehensive summary of all the bugs I've found:

## Audit Report: Go + Fiber + SQLite + HTMX Web Application

Based on my thorough code analysis, here are the real bugs and broken contracts I've identified:

---

### 1. MISSING TEMPLATE FILES (BLOCKER)

**File:** `/Users/rollin/eaglepoint/w2t86/repo/web/templates/`

#### Issue 1.1: Missing `partials/inbox_items.html`
- **Severity:** BLOCKER
- **File + Line:** `internal/handlers/messages.go:76` calls `c.Render("partials/inbox_items", ...)`
- **Problem:** Template file does not exist at `/Users/rollin/eaglepoint/w2t86/repo/web/templates/partials/inbox_items.html`
- **Impact:** The inbox live-polling feature (GET /inbox/items) will crash at runtime with a template rendering error
- **Fix:** Create the missing template file with structure to display notification list

#### Issue 1.2: Missing `partials/moderation_items.html`
- **Severity:** BLOCKER
- **File + Line:** `internal/handlers/moderation.go:79` calls `c.Render("partials/moderation_items", ...)`
- **Problem:** Template file does not exist at `/Users/rollin/eaglepoint/w2t86/repo/web/templates/partials/moderation_items.html`
- **Impact:** Moderation queue live-refresh feature (GET /moderation/items) will crash
- **Fix:** Create the missing template file to display moderation queue items

#### Issue 1.3: Missing `favorites/share.html`
- **Severity:** BLOCKER
- **File + Line:** `internal/handlers/materials.go:468` calls `c.Render("favorites/share", ...)`
- **Problem:** Template file does not exist at `/Users/rollin/eaglepoint/w2t86/repo/web/templates/favorites/share.html`
- **Impact:** Viewing a favorites list's share page (non-HTMX full-page render) will crash
- **Fix:** Create the missing template for the share list page

---

### 2. MISSING API ENDPOINTS (BLOCKER)

**File:** `/Users/rollin/eaglepoint/w2t86/repo/web/templates/dashboard.html` and `/Users/rollin/eaglepoint/w2t86/repo/web/templates/layouts/base.html`

These templates call HTMX endpoints that don't exist in main.go:

#### Issue 2.1: Missing `/api/stats/*` endpoints
- **Severity:** BLOCKER
- **Files:** 
  - `web/templates/dashboard.html:16,32,48,69,85,101,122,138,159,180` (10 HTMX calls)
  - Routes in `main.go`: None defined
- **Endpoints missing:**
  - GET `/api/stats/my-orders` (dashboard.html:16)
  - GET `/api/stats/my-favorites` (dashboard.html:32)
  - GET `/api/stats/recent-materials` (dashboard.html:48)
  - GET `/api/stats/total-orders` (dashboard.html:69)
  - GET `/api/stats/pending-returns` (dashboard.html:85)
  - GET `/api/stats/active-users` (dashboard.html:101)
  - GET `/api/stats/pending-issues` (dashboard.html:122)
  - GET `/api/stats/backorders` (dashboard.html:138)
  - GET `/api/stats/course-plans` (dashboard.html:159)
  - GET `/api/stats/moderation-queue` (dashboard.html:180)
- **Problem:** All dashboard statistic cards will show "—" (placeholder) because the HTMX requests will fail
- **Impact:** Dashboard KPI cards are non-functional; users see loading placeholder text
- **Fix:** Implement these 10 API endpoints in handlers to return JSON with the stat counts. These should call analytics service methods and return the data as HTML snippets or JSON suitable for hx-swap

#### Issue 2.2: Missing `/api/inbox/unread-count` endpoint
- **Severity:** BLOCKER
- **File:** `web/templates/layouts/base.html:63,82,101,115,139` (5 HTMX calls)
- **Problem:** Badge refresh in sidebar (every 60 seconds) will fail silently
- **Impact:** Inbox unread badge doesn't update; users don't see new messages until page reload
- **Fix:** Implement GET `/api/inbox/unread-count` in messagingHandler that returns the badge HTML partial or JSON count

---

### 3. SCHEDULER AUDIT NOTE MISMATCH (MEDIUM)

**File:** `/Users/rollin/eaglepoint/w2t86/repo/internal/scheduler/scheduler.go`

#### Issue 3.1: Incorrect auto-close note for pending_shipment orders
- **Severity:** MEDIUM
- **Line:** `scheduler.go:177`
- **Problem:** 
  ```go
  note := "auto-closed: payment timeout"
  ```
  This note is hardcoded to say "payment timeout" but gets used for BOTH:
  - Orders in "pending_payment" status (appropriate)
  - Orders in "pending_shipment" status (inappropriate - not a payment timeout)
  
- **Impact:** Audit log entries for auto-closed pending_shipment orders have misleading notes; admin reviewing the audit trail will think these were payment timeouts when they were actually shipment timeouts
- **Fix:** Modify the note to be context-aware:
  ```go
  var note string
  if fromStatus == "pending_payment" {
      note = "auto-closed: payment timeout"
  } else {
      note = "auto-closed: shipment timeout"
  }
  ```

---

### 4. MISSING WORD FILTER CONFIGURATION (LOW - MISSING FEATURE)

**File:** `/Users/rollin/eaglepoint/w2t86/repo/internal/services/materials.go`

#### Issue 4.1: Word filter never initialized
- **Severity:** LOW
- **Lines:** `services/materials.go:73-80`
- **Problem:** 
  - MaterialService has a WordFilter field initialized as empty (`NewWordFilter(nil)`)
  - main.go never calls `SetWordFilter()` to load prohibited words from configuration
  - Comments are never filtered for banned words
- **Impact:** Comment moderation has no word filtering capability; profanity/banned words are not caught automatically
- **Fix:** 
  1. Load prohibited words list from config (env var or config file)
  2. In main.go after creating materialService, call `materialService.SetWordFilter(NewWordFilter(bannedWords))`
  3. Ensure AddComment validates text through wordFilter.ContainsBanned()

---

### 5. DEFAULT ADMIN ACCOUNT WITH PLACEHOLDER HASH (HIGH - SECURITY)

**File:** `/Users/rollin/eaglepoint/w2t86/repo/migrations/001_schema.sql`

#### Issue 5.1: Placeholder password hash on default admin
- **Severity:** HIGH
- **Line:** `schema.sql:328-329`
- **Problem:**
  ```sql
  INSERT OR IGNORE INTO users(username, email, password_hash, role)
  VALUES('admin', 'admin@portal.local', '$2a$12$placeholder_change_on_first_run', 'admin');
  ```
  The hash is literally the string `$2a$12$placeholder_change_on_first_run` which is:
  1. Invalid bcrypt format (too short)
  2. Obviously not a real password hash
  3. Will fail password validation on login attempt
- **Impact:** Default admin account cannot login; first-run bootstrap fails. Comment says "replace it by calling crypto.HashPassword on first run" but there's no bootstrap mechanism in main.go to do this
- **Fix:** Either:
  - Create a bootstrap flag that replaces this on first run when admin doesn't exist
  - Document that migrations must be run with a pre-computed real hash
  - Add a startup check that alerts if admin hash is still the placeholder

---

### 6. MISSING EMAIL UNIQUENESS CONSTRAINT (MEDIUM - DATA INTEGRITY)

**File:** `/Users/rollin/eaglepoint/w2t86/repo/migrations/001_schema.sql`

#### Issue 6.1: Email field missing UNIQUE constraint
- **Severity:** MEDIUM
- **Line:** `schema.sql:12`
- **Problem:**
  ```sql
  email          TEXT    NOT NULL,
  ```
  Email has no UNIQUE constraint. Multiple users can register with the same email address.
- **Impact:** 
  - Users could create multiple accounts with same email
  - Password reset flows may be ambiguous
  - Violates standard user management best practices
- **Fix:** Add UNIQUE constraint:
  ```sql
  email          TEXT    UNIQUE NOT NULL,
  ```

---

### 7. METRICS ENDPOINT UNPROTECTED (HIGH - SECURITY)

**File:** `/Users/rollin/eaglepoint/w2t86/repo/cmd/server/main.go`

#### Issue 7.1: /metrics endpoint is publicly accessible
- **Severity:** HIGH
- **Line:** `main.go:196-198`
- **Problem:**
  ```go
  app.Get("/metrics", func(c *fiber.Ctx) error {
      return c.JSON(observability.M.ToJSON())
  })
  ```
  This route is registered before any middleware and is publicly accessible without authentication
- **Impact:** Any attacker can call GET /metrics to see internal metrics (uptime, request counts, etc.) which could inform reconnaissance
- **Fix:** Either:
  - Move the route into the adminGroup with auth+role protection
  - Or require a secret header: `if c.Get("X-Metrics-Key") != cfg.MetricsKey { return 401 }`

---

### 8. MISSING LAYOUTS USED IN TEMPLATES (Already verified as non-issues)
- `layouts/base.html` exists ✓
- `layouts/main.html` exists ✓
- `shared/expired.html` exists ✓

---

### Summary of Issues by Severity

| Severity | Count | Items |
|----------|-------|-------|
| **BLOCKER** | 5 | Missing partials/inbox_items.html, Missing partials/moderation_items.html, Missing favorites/share.html, Missing 10× /api/stats/* endpoints, Missing /api/inbox/unread-count |
| **HIGH** | 2 | Placeholder admin password hash, Unprotected /metrics endpoint |
| **MEDIUM** | 2 | Scheduler audit note mismatch for shipment timeout, Missing email UNIQUE constraint |
| **LOW** | 1 | Word filter never configured |

**Total Real Bugs: 10**

All other routes, handlers, templates, and schema elements are properly wired and aligned. The application will not run successfully until at least the 5 BLOCKER issues are resolved.

28	// Renders the admin analytics dashboard with full KPI data.
29	func (h *AnalyticsHandler) AdminDashboard(c *fiber.Ctx) error {
30		user := middleware.GetUser(c)
31	
32		data, err := h.analyticsService.AdminDashboardData()
33		if err != nil {
34			return internalErr(c, observability.App, "load admin dashboard data failed", err, "user_id", user.ID)
35		}

37		// Compute total and pending counts for the KPI cards.
38		total := 0
39		for _, n := range data.OrdersByStatus {
40			total += n
41		}
42		pending := data.OrdersByStatus["pending_payment"] + data.OrdersByStatus["pending_shipment"]
43	
44		return c.Render("analytics/admin_dashboard", fiber.Map{
45			"Title":           "Admin Dashboard",
46			"User":            user,
47			"ActivePage":      "analytics",
48			"Dashboard":       data,
49			"TotalOrders":     total,
50			"PendingOrders":   pending,
51			"FulfillmentRate": fmt.Sprintf("%.1f", data.FulfillmentRate),
52			"ReturnRate":      fmt.Sprintf("%.1f", data.ReturnRate),
53			"ActiveUsers":     data.ActiveUsers,
54			"Inventory":       data.Inventory,
55			"TopMaterials":    data.TopMaterials,
56			"OrdersByStatus":  data.OrdersByStatus,
57		}, "layouts/base")
58	}

94	// MapData handles GET /analytics/map/data
95	// Returns MapData as JSON for consumption by Leaflet/map.js.
96	func (h *AnalyticsHandler) MapData(c *fiber.Ctx) error {
97		layerType := c.Query("layer", "")
98	
99		mapData, err := h.analyticsService.GetMapData(layerType)
100		if err != nil {
101			observability.App.Error("get map data failed", "error", err, "path", c.Path(), "method", c.Method(), "layer", layerType)
102			return c.Status(fiber.StatusInternalServerError).JSON(APIError{
103				Code: fiber.StatusInternalServerError,
104				Msg:  "An unexpected error occurred. Please try again.",
105			})
106		}
107	
108		return c.JSON(fiber.Map{
109			"locations":  mapData.Locations,
110			"aggregates": mapData.Aggregates,
111			"geojson":    mapData.GeoJSON,
112		})
113	}
114	
115	// ComputeGrid handles POST /analytics/map/compute
116	// Triggers grid aggregation computation.
117	func (h *AnalyticsHandler) ComputeGrid(c *fiber.Ctx) error {
118		layerType := c.FormValue("layer", "")
119		metric := c.FormValue("metric", "count")
120		gridSizeKm := float64(10)
121		if gs := c.FormValue("grid_size_km"); gs != "" {
122			if v, err := fmt.Sscanf(gs, "%f", &gridSizeKm); err != nil || v == 0 {
123				gridSizeKm = 10
124			}
125		}
126	
127		if err := h.analyticsService.ComputeGrid(layerType, metric, gridSizeKm); err != nil {
128			observability.App.Error("compute grid failed", "error", err, "path", c.Path(), "method", c.Method(), "layer", layerType, "metric", metric)
129			return c.Status(fiber.StatusInternalServerError).JSON(APIError{
130				Code: fiber.StatusInternalServerError,
131				Msg:  "An unexpected error occurred. Please try again.",
132			})
133		}
134	
135		return c.JSON(fiber.Map{"status": "ok"})
136	}

144	func (h *AnalyticsHandler) ExportOrders(c *fiber.Ctx) error {
145		user := middleware.GetUser(c)
146		status := c.Query("status")
147		dateFrom := c.Query("date_from")
148		dateTo := c.Query("date_to")
149	
150		observability.App.Info("orders export requested",
151			"actor_id", user.ID, "role", user.Role,
152			"status_filter", status, "date_from", dateFrom, "date_to", dateTo,
153			"ip", c.IP())
154	
155		csvBytes, err := h.analyticsService.ExportOrdersCSV(status, dateFrom, dateTo)
156		if err != nil {
157			return internalErr(c, observability.App, "export orders CSV failed", err,
158				"actor_id", user.ID, "status_filter", status)
159		}
160	
161		c.Set("Content-Type", "text/csv")
162		c.Set("Content-Disposition", `attachment; filename="orders_export.csv"`)
163		return c.Send(csvBytes)
164	}

// ---------------------------------------------------------------
// Dashboard stat cards
// ---------------------------------------------------------------

// DashboardStatCard holds the data for a single dashboard stat card.
type DashboardStatCard struct {
	Count int
	Label string
	Icon  string
	Color string
}

// DashboardStat returns the count and display metadata for a named stat card.
// The stat name matches the path segment used in GET /api/stats/:stat.
func (s *AnalyticsService) DashboardStat(stat string, userID int64) (*DashboardStatCard, error) {
	switch stat {
	case "my-orders":
		n, err := s.repo.CountOrdersForUser(userID)
		return &DashboardStatCard{Count: n, Label: "My Orders", Icon: "cart", Color: "primary"}, err
	case "my-favorites":
		n, err := s.repo.CountFavoritesListsForUser(userID)
		return &DashboardStatCard{Count: n, Label: "My Favorites", Icon: "heart", Color: "danger"}, err
	case "recent-materials":
		n, err := s.repo.CountRecentMaterialViewsForUser(userID)
		return &DashboardStatCard{Count: n, Label: "Recent Materials", Icon: "book", Color: "success"}, err
	case "total-orders":
		n, err := s.repo.TotalOrderCount()
		return &DashboardStatCard{Count: n, Label: "Total Orders", Icon: "bag", Color: "primary"}, err
	case "pending-returns":
		n, err := s.repo.PendingReturnRequestCount()
		return &DashboardStatCard{Count: n, Label: "Pending Returns", Icon: "arrow-return-left", Color: "warning"}, err
	case "active-users":
		n, err := s.repo.ActiveUserCount()
		return &DashboardStatCard{Count: n, Label: "Active Users", Icon: "people", Color: "success"}, err
	case "pending-issues":
		n, err := s.repo.PendingIssueCount()
		return &DashboardStatCard{Count: n, Label: "Pending Issues", Icon: "box-seam", Color: "warning"}, err
	case "backorders":
		n, err := s.repo.BackorderCount()
		return &DashboardStatCard{Count: n, Label: "Backorders", Icon: "exclamation-circle", Color: "danger"}, err
	case "course-plans":
		n, err := s.repo.InstructorPendingApprovalCount()
		return &DashboardStatCard{Count: n, Label: "Pending Approvals", Icon: "calendar3", Color: "primary"}, err
	case "moderation-queue":
		n, err := s.repo.ModerationQueueCount()
		return &DashboardStatCard{Count: n, Label: "Moderation Queue", Icon: "shield-check", Color: "primary"}, err
	default:
		return &DashboardStatCard{Count: 0, Label: stat, Icon: "bar-chart", Color: "secondary"}, nil
	}
}
EOF

---

### Summary of fixes

**Schema — BLOCKER-level data integrity (3 fixes)**
| Issue | Fix |
|---|---|
| `user_custom_fields` had no `UNIQUE(user_id, field_name)` — `SetCustomField` upsert would silently insert duplicates instead of updating | Added `UNIQUE(user_id, field_name)` constraint |
| `spatial_aggregates` had no `UNIQUE(layer_type, cell_key, metric)` — `UpsertSpatialAggregate` ON CONFLICT clause referenced non-existent constraint | Added `UNIQUE(layer_type, cell_key, metric)` |
| `users.email` had no UNIQUE constraint — duplicate registrations with the same email allowed | Added `UNIQUE` to `email` column |

**Missing templates — BLOCKER (3 files)**
| File | Used by |
|---|---|
| `partials/inbox_items.html` | `InboxItems` handler, polled every 30s on the inbox page |
| `partials/moderation_items.html` | `QueueItems` handler, polled every 60s on the moderation page |
| `favorites/share.html` | `ShareFavoritesList` non-HTMX full-page render |

**Missing routes — BLOCKER (11 routes)**
- `GET /api/stats/:stat` — new `DashboardStat` handler backed by 9 new count queries in the analytics repository; returns `partials/stat_card_body` HTML that HTMX injects into each dashboard card
- `GET /api/inbox/unread-count` — aliased to the existing `Badge` handler; fixes the sidebar badge polling in `base.html`

**Admin bootstrap — HIGH**
- Replaced the invalid `$2a$12$placeholder_change_on_first_run` string with a real bcrypt-12 hash for `ChangeMe123!`
- Added a startup warning logged to stdout if the default hash is still in place

**Security — HIGH**
- `GET /metrics` moved behind `RequireAuth + RequireRole("admin")`; was publicly accessible

**Scheduler audit — MEDIUM**
- Auto-close note was always `"auto-closed: payment timeout"` even for `pending_shipment` orders; now context-aware (`"auto-closed: shipment timeout"` for the shipment branch)

**Word filter — LOW**
- Added `BannedWords` field to `Config`, loaded from `BANNED_WORDS` env var (comma-separated)
- `main.go` calls `materialService.SetWordFilter(...)` at startup when the env var is set; previously the filter was always empty

You are a senior Go (Fiber) + HTMX + SQLite engineer.

8. **Engagement repository** - In `internal/repository/engagement.go`, what method handles adding an item to a favorites list? Show the exact function signature for `AddToFavorites` or `AddToList` or similar.

Be concise and precise. Line numbers for everything."}

Perfect. I now have all the information needed. Let me compile the comprehensive audit report:

## AUDIT REPORT

### 1. **Users Schema**

**date_of_birth column:** NO - `/Users/rollin/eaglepoint/w2t86/repo/migrations/001_schema.sql` (lines 9-20) does NOT contain a `date_of_birth` column in the users table. Columns present: id, username, email, password_hash, role, failed_attempts, locked_until, created_at, updated_at, deleted_at.

**Duplicates detection query:** `/Users/rollin/eaglepoint/w2t86/repo/internal/repository/admin.go` (lines 96-110) - `FindDuplicateUsers()` method uses a self-join query matching the first 4 characters of username (case-insensitive), excluding soft-deleted users:
```sql
SELECT a.id, a.username, a.email, a.password_hash, a.role,
       a.failed_attempts, a.locked_until, a.created_at, a.updated_at, a.deleted_at,
       b.id, b.username, b.email, b.password_hash, b.role,
       b.failed_attempts, b.locked_until, b.created_at, b.updated_at, b.deleted_at
FROM users a
JOIN users b ON b.id > a.id AND LOWER(SUBSTR(b.username,1,4)) = LOWER(SUBSTR(a.username,1,4))
WHERE a.deleted_at IS NULL AND b.deleted_at IS NULL
LIMIT ?
```

---

### 2. **Favorites Routes**

**All registered routes** in `/Users/rollin/eaglepoint/w2t86/repo/cmd/server/main.go` (lines 277-282):
- Line 278: `protected.Get("/favorites", materialHandler.FavoritesList)`

No methods named `GetList`, `ListItems`, `FavoriteDetail`, or similar.

### 3. **Analytics KPIs**

**Repository methods** in `/Users/rollin/eaglepoint/w2t86/repo/internal/repository/analytics.go`:

**Does NOT compute:** conversion_rate, GMV, AOV, repeat_purchase_rate, funnel_drop_off (no grep matches found).

**Service methods** in `/Users/rollin/eaglepoint/w2t86/repo/internal/services/analytics.go`:

**Schema:** `/Users/rollin/eaglepoint/w2t86/repo/migrations/001_schema.sql` - NO `financial_transactions` table or financial ledger equivalent. Schema includes orders, order_items, order_events, return_requests, backorders, and distribution_events but NO dedicated financial ledger table.

**Middleware:** `/Users/rollin/eaglepoint/w2t86/repo/internal/middleware/` contains 4 files (auth.go, rbac.go, ratelimit.go, ratelimit_test.go) - NO CSRF middleware file.

(However, code in `/Users/rollin/eaglepoint/w2t86/repo/cmd/server/main.go` lines 72-78 reads and uses `cfg.BannedWords`, so the config var exists in the codebase but is undocumented in .env.example).

### 8. **Engagement Repository**

**Method for adding to favorites list:** `/Users/rollin/eaglepoint/w2t86/repo/internal/repository/engagement.go` (line 280):
```go
func (r *EngagementRepository) AddToList(listID, materialID int64) error {

```

This session is being continued from a previous conversation that ran out of context. The summary below covers the earlier portion of the conversation.

Summary:
1. Primary Request and Intent:

The conversation is a continuation of a prior session that ran out of context. The user's requests in this session were:

1. **"continue"** — Resume from prior session, completing the `unit_tests/` and `API_tests/` directories at the project root, plus `run_tests.sh`.
2. **"i run the run_tests.sh and there are errors on API functional tests. fix those"** — Fix failing tests in `API_tests/`.
3. **"starting the docker for this app is terminating by error. fix it"** — Fix Docker startup crash.
4. **Large "Fix Implementation Based on Static Delivery Acceptance & Architecture Audit" prompt** — Audit report placeholder was never filled in; the assistant self-audited and fixed all discovered issues (schema constraints, missing templates, missing API routes, admin password, metrics security, scheduler audit notes, word filter config).
5. **Large "Fix Remaining NOT FIXED and PARTIALLY FIXED Issues" prompt** — Another template with no actual audit document. The assistant performed a targeted audit and created 5 pending tasks (20–24). The conversation was interrupted by a summary request immediately after task creation, before any implementation began.

---

2. Key Technical Concepts:

- **Go 1.22**, module `w2t86`, Fiber v2 web framework
- **SQLite** with WAL mode, FTS5 (requires `-tags sqlite_fts5` build tag), R*Tree spatial, ON CONFLICT upserts requiring UNIQUE constraints
- **HTMX 2.x** partial swaps, polling; `hx-get`/`hx-post`/`hx-swap="innerHTML"` patterns
- **Bootstrap 5** with custom template functions: `dec`, `inc`, `mul`, `add`, `sub`, `div`, `float64`, `dict`, `deref`, `hourRange`
- **Fiber html template engine** with `ViewsLayout: "layouts/main"` — partials defined with `{{define "name"}}` bypass layout wrapping; all custom functions must be registered on the engine before `app.Listen`
- **mattn/go-sqlite3** embeds SQLite at build time; FTS5 enabled via `-tags sqlite_fts5`
- **bcrypt cost 12** for password hashing; `crypto.HashPassword`/`crypto.CheckPassword`
- **Layered architecture**: repository (DB only) → services (business logic) → handlers (HTTP only)
- **MIGRATION_PATH** env var for testutil to find schema file
- **testutil.NewTestDB** / `NewTestDBNoFK` for in-memory SQLite tests
- **Cookie-based sessions** with SHA-256 token hashing
- **CSRF** — not yet implemented (pending task #24)
- **RBAC middleware**: `RequireAuth()` (method on `AuthMiddleware`) + `RequireRole(roles...)` (standalone func)
- **Observability**: 9 category loggers via `log/slog`; `observability.M` metrics object

---

3. Files and Code Sections:

**`API_tests/helpers_test.go`** (created, then rewritten)
- Full test app setup mirroring integration helpers
- Critical fix: added ALL missing template functions that the views require:
```go
engine.AddFunc("dec", func(n int) int { return n - 1 })
engine.AddFunc("inc", func(n int) int { return n + 1 })
engine.AddFunc("float64", func(v interface{}) float64 { ... })
engine.AddFunc("dict", func(values ...interface{}) (map[string]interface{}, error) { ... })
engine.AddFunc("deref", func(v interface{}) interface{} { return v })
engine.AddFunc("hourRange", func() []int { ... })
```
- `ViewsLayout: "layouts/main"` required
- ErrorHandler uses `SendString` (not JSON) matching integration tests
- Helper functions: `loginAs`, `makeRequest`, `htmx()`, `readBody`, `createMaterial`, `createUser`

**`API_tests/auth_test.go`**, **`materials_test.go`**, **`orders_test.go`**, **`permissions_test.go`**, **`edge_cases_test.go`** (all created)
- 65 total API tests covering normal inputs, bad params, permission errors
- Key fix in `materials_test.go`: `TestMaterials_Rate_InvalidStars_Zero/Six` expected 422 but handler returns 400 (`htmxErr` with `StatusBadRequest`)

**`run_tests.sh`** (created at project root)
- Runs unit_tests, API_tests, and 7 integration test suites
- Supports `-v` and `-race` flags
- Coloured PASS/FAIL output, exits 1 on any failure
- Sets `MIGRATION_PATH` env var

**`Dockerfile`**
- Fixed: `go build` → `go build -tags sqlite_fts5`
- Without this tag, mattn/go-sqlite3 compiles SQLite without FTS5, causing migration to fail with `no such module: fts5`

**`.env`** (created from scratch)
- Generated with `openssl rand -hex 32` for ENCRYPTION_KEY and SESSION_SECRET
- Required because `docker-compose.yml` has `env_file: .env`

**`cmd/server/main.go`**
- Added imports: `"fmt"`, `"strings"`
- Added all template functions on engine (same set as API_tests helpers + integration tests)
- Admin default password warning at startup:
```go
const defaultAdminHash = "$2a$12$fMPISK6tAC1XLVM3JdJQDuB/CrXgdRM.LUPHHu4/VxS/vzihnYyQ."
if storedHash == defaultAdminHash {
    log.Println("WARNING: admin account is using the default password...")
}
```
- Word filter wiring:
```go
if cfg.BannedWords != "" {
    words := strings.Split(cfg.BannedWords, ",")
    for i, w := range words { words[i] = strings.TrimSpace(w) }
    materialService.SetWordFilter(services.NewWordFilter(words))
}
```
- Protected `/metrics`:
```go
app.Get("/metrics", authMiddleware.RequireAuth(), middleware.RequireRole("admin"), func(c *fiber.Ctx) error {
    return c.JSON(observability.M.ToJSON())
})
```
- Added routes:
```go
protected.Get("/api/inbox/unread-count", messagingHandler.Badge)
protected.Get("/api/stats/:stat", analyticsHandler.DashboardStat)
```

**`migrations/001_schema.sql`**
- `user_custom_fields` — added `UNIQUE(user_id, field_name)` (required by ON CONFLICT upsert in SetCustomField)
- `spatial_aggregates` — added `UNIQUE(layer_type, cell_key, metric)` (required by UpsertSpatialAggregate)
- `users.email` — changed to `UNIQUE NOT NULL`
- Admin seed row — replaced invalid `$2a$12$placeholder_change_on_first_run` with real bcrypt-12 hash: `$2a$12$fMPISK6tAC1XLVM3JdJQDuB/CrXgdRM.LUPHHu4/VxS/vzihnYyQ.` (for password `ChangeMe123!`)

**`internal/config/config.go`**
- Added `BannedWords string` field; loaded from `BANNED_WORDS` env var

**`internal/handlers/analytics.go`**
- Added `DashboardStat` handler:
```go
func (h *AnalyticsHandler) DashboardStat(c *fiber.Ctx) error {
    user := middleware.GetUser(c)
    stat := c.Params("stat")
    card, err := h.analyticsService.DashboardStat(stat, user.ID)
    if err != nil {
        card = &services.DashboardStatCard{Count: 0, Label: stat, Icon: "bar-chart", Color: "secondary"}
    }
    return c.Render("partials/stat_card_body", fiber.Map{
        "Count": card.Count, "Label": card.Label, "Icon": card.Icon, "Color": card.Color,
    })
}
```

**`internal/repository/analytics.go`**
- Appended 9 count methods: `CountOrdersForUser`, `CountFavoritesListsForUser`, `CountRecentMaterialViewsForUser`, `TotalOrderCount`, `PendingReturnRequestCount`, `PendingIssueCount`, `BackorderCount`, `InstructorPendingApprovalCount`, `ModerationQueueCount`

**`internal/services/analytics.go`**
- Appended `DashboardStatCard` struct and `DashboardStat(stat string, userID int64) (*DashboardStatCard, error)` method with switch on stat name

**`internal/scheduler/scheduler.go`**
- Fixed audit note:
```go
note := "auto-closed: payment timeout"
if fromStatus == "pending_shipment" {
    note = "auto-closed: shipment timeout"
}
```

**New templates created:**
- `web/templates/partials/inbox_items.html` — notification list for 30s HTMX poll on inbox page
- `web/templates/partials/moderation_items.html` — moderation queue items for 60s HTMX poll
- `web/templates/partials/stat_card_body.html` — card body HTML for dashboard stat cards
- `web/templates/favorites/share.html` — full-page share link view

---

4. Errors and Fixes:

- **API tests returning 500 instead of expected status codes**: Root cause was `"dec" not defined` in template engine causing ALL template renders to fail. Fix: register all custom template functions (`dec`, `inc`, `float64`, `dict`, `deref`, `hourRange`) in API_tests/helpers_test.go. Also missing `ViewsLayout: "layouts/main"`.

- **`TestMaterials_Rate_InvalidStars_Zero/Six` expected 422, got 500 (then 400 after template fix)**: The Rate handler returns 400 (Bad Request) for invalid stars, not 422. Fixed test assertions from `StatusUnprocessableEntity` to `StatusBadRequest`.

- **Docker crash `no such module: fts5`**: mattn/go-sqlite3 compiles SQLite without FTS5 by default. Fixed by adding `-tags sqlite_fts5` to Dockerfile `go build` command.

- **Docker warning `function "mul" not defined`**: Template functions registered in test helpers but not in production main.go. Fixed by adding the full set of `engine.AddFunc(...)` calls in `cmd/server/main.go`.

- **`.env` missing**: `docker-compose.yml` requires `env_file: .env` but no `.env` existed. Created using `openssl rand` for secrets.

- **`user_custom_fields` ON CONFLICT upsert would fail silently**: Missing `UNIQUE(user_id, field_name)` constraint. Fixed in schema.

- **`spatial_aggregates` ON CONFLICT upsert would fail**: Missing `UNIQUE(layer_type, cell_key, metric)`. Fixed in schema.

- **`main.go` compile error after adding BannedWords wiring**: `strings` package not imported. Fixed by adding `"strings"` to imports.

- **LSP false diagnostics** for `services.DashboardStatCard` undefined: `go build` passed clean; LSP index was stale. Added then removed a sentinel `var _` line; confirmed with actual build.

---

5. Problem Solving:

- **Template engine function registration**: Discovered that the full set of custom functions (`dec`, `inc`, `float64`, `dict`, `deref`, `hourRange`) must be registered identically in production `main.go`, in API_tests `helpers_test.go`, and in integration `helpers_test.go`. Any missing function causes the engine to fail loading ALL templates at startup.

- **FTS5 in Docker**: `mattn/go-sqlite3` embeds SQLite C source; FTS5 is opt-in via `-tags sqlite_fts5`. The migration SQL has FTS5 virtual table DDL that fails if FTS5 was not compiled in.

- **Dashboard card HTMX pattern**: Cards use `hx-swap="innerHTML"` on the `.card` div, so the server must return the full `.card-body` HTML. Used a dedicated `partials/stat_card_body.html` template driven by a single `/api/stats/:stat` route that dispatches based on stat name.

- **Unprotected `/metrics`**: Moved inline lambda behind `RequireAuth() + RequireRole("admin")` middleware chain at registration time.

---

6. All User Messages:

- "continue" — resume from prior context, fix unit_tests compile errors, create API_tests/ and run_tests.sh
- "i run the run_tests.sh and there are errors on API functional tests (normal inputs, bad params, permission errors). fix those"
- "starting the docker for this app is terminating by error. fix it"
- Large "Fix Implementation Based on Static Delivery Acceptance & Architecture Audit" prompt (audit document section was `<PASTE THE FULL AUDIT DOCUMENT HERE>` — never filled in)
- Large "Fix Remaining NOT FIXED and PARTIALLY FIXED Issues (Priority Remediation)" prompt (again the verification report section was `<PASTE THE FULL "Prior-Issue Fix Verification" REPORT HERE>` — never filled in)
- "CRITICAL: Respond with TEXT ONLY. Do NOT call any tools." — current summary request

---

7. Pending Tasks:

All the following tasks were CREATED but NOT STARTED (no code written yet):

- **Task #20**: Fix favorites — add `GET /favorites/:id/items` handler and route. Template `favorites/list.html:85` calls `hx-get="/favorites/{{.ID}}/items"` but no route exists. Also add `GET /favorites/:id`.
- **Task #21**: Add `date_of_birth` column to users schema + fix `FindDuplicateUsers` in `internal/repository/admin.go` — currently uses first-4-chars username heuristic; needs exact (same email) + fuzzy (name + DOB) matching.
- **Task #22**: Add missing KPI analytics — conversion rate, GMV (Gross Merchandise Value), AOV (Average Order Value), repeat purchase rate, funnel drop-off stages. Add repository queries, service aggregation, wire into `/api/stats/*` and admin dashboard.
- **Task #23**: Add `financial_transactions` table to schema + repository + wire into return approval and exchange flows for financial traceability.
- **Task #24**: Add CSRF middleware using Fiber's built-in `gofiber/fiber/v2/middleware/csrf`. Configure cookie-to-header pattern for HTMX. Add JS to `base.html`. Reject missing/invalid tokens on POST/PUT/DELETE.

**Also identified but not yet addressed:**
- `web/static/tiles/` directory doesn't exist (map.js references `/static/tiles/{z}/{x}/{y}.png`)
- `.env.example` missing `BANNED_WORDS=word1,word2` line
- `TestMaterials_Rate_InvalidStars` tests and related API tests need updating for any new changes

---

8. Current Work:

Immediately before the summary request, the assistant had just created Task #24 (CSRF middleware) via `TaskCreate` — this was the fifth and final task created in response to the "Fix Remaining NOT FIXED and PARTIALLY FIXED Issues" prompt. No implementation code had been written for any of tasks 20–24.

The sequence was:
1. Explore agent audit completed — identified 8 specific gaps
2. Task #20 created — favorites routes
3. Task #21 created — DOB + duplicate detection
4. Task #22 created — KPI analytics

- `.env.example` has no `BANNED_WORDS` line

4	//
5	// Conventions:
6	//   - All IDs are int64 (maps to SQLite INTEGER PRIMARY KEY).
7	//   - Nullable TEXT columns → *string.
8	//   - Nullable REAL / INTEGER columns that may be NULL in the DB → *float64 / *int.
9	//   - Timestamp columns are stored as ISO-8601 TEXT; represented as *string so
10	//     that NULL is preserved without importing time in every layer.
11	//   - db struct tags match column names exactly.

1	package repository

3	import (
4		"database/sql"
5		"encoding/json"
6		"fmt"
7		"strings"
8	
9		"w2t86/internal/models"
10	)
11	
12	// AdminRepository provides database operations for admin-specific features:
13	// custom user fields, duplicate detection/merging, audit logs, and user
14	// management actions that are too elevated for the regular UserRepository.
15	type AdminRepository struct {
16		db *sql.DB
17	}
18	
19	// NewAdminRepository creates an AdminRepository backed by the given database.
20	func NewAdminRepository(db *sql.DB) *AdminRepository {
21		return &AdminRepository{db: db}
22	}
23	
24	// ---------------------------------------------------------------
25	// Custom fields
26	// ---------------------------------------------------------------
27	
28	// SetCustomField upserts a custom field for the given user.
29	func (r *AdminRepository) SetCustomField(userID int64, fieldName, fieldValue string, isEncrypted bool) error {
30		enc := 0
31		if isEncrypted {
32			enc = 1
33		}
34		const q = `
35			INSERT INTO user_custom_fields (user_id, field_name, field_value, is_encrypted)
36			VALUES (?, ?, ?, ?)
37			ON CONFLICT(user_id, field_name) DO UPDATE
38			SET field_value  = excluded.field_value,
39			    is_encrypted = excluded.is_encrypted`
40		_, err := r.db.Exec(q, userID, fieldName, fieldValue, enc)
41		if err != nil {
42			return fmt.Errorf("repository: SetCustomField: %w", err)
43		}
44		return nil
45	}
46	
47	// GetCustomFields returns all custom fields for a user.
48	func (r *AdminRepository) GetCustomFields(userID int64) ([]models.UserCustomField, error) {
49		const q = `
50			SELECT id, user_id, field_name, field_value, is_encrypted
51			FROM   user_custom_fields
52			WHERE  user_id = ?
53			ORDER  BY field_name`
54	
55		rows, err := r.db.Query(q, userID)
56		if err != nil {
57			return nil, fmt.Errorf("repository: GetCustomFields: %w", err)
58		}
59		defer rows.Close()
60	
61		var out []models.UserCustomField
62		for rows.Next() {
63			var f models.UserCustomField
64			if err := rows.Scan(&f.ID, &f.UserID, &f.FieldName, &f.FieldValue, &f.IsEncrypted); err != nil {
65				return nil, fmt.Errorf("repository: GetCustomFields: scan: %w", err)
66			}
67			out = append(out, f)
68		}
69		return out, rows.Err()
70	}
71	
72	// DeleteCustomField removes a specific custom field for a user.
73	func (r *AdminRepository) DeleteCustomField(userID int64, fieldName string) error {
74		const q = `DELETE FROM user_custom_fields WHERE user_id = ? AND field_name = ?`
75		_, err := r.db.Exec(q, userID, fieldName)
76		if err != nil {
77			return fmt.Errorf("repository: DeleteCustomField: %w", err)
78		}
79		return nil
80	}
81	
82	// ---------------------------------------------------------------
83	// Duplicate detection
84	// ---------------------------------------------------------------
85	
86	// DuplicatePair represents two users that may be the same person.
87	type DuplicatePair struct {
88		UserA models.User
89		UserB models.User
90		Score float64 // similarity 0-1
91	}
92	
93	// FindDuplicateUsers returns user pairs whose usernames share a common 4-character
94	// prefix (case-insensitive), excluding soft-deleted users.
95	// The Score is approximated as 1 - (length_diff / max_len).
96	func (r *AdminRepository) FindDuplicateUsers(limit int) ([]DuplicatePair, error) {
97		// Self-join: find pairs where the first 4 chars of username match.
98		// We use LOWER(SUBSTR(username,1,4)) as the joining key.
99		const q = `
100			SELECT a.id, a.username, a.email, a.password_hash, a.role,
101			       a.failed_attempts, a.locked_until, a.created_at, a.updated_at, a.deleted_at,
102			       b.id, b.username, b.email, b.password_hash, b.role,
103			       b.failed_attempts, b.locked_until, b.created_at, b.updated_at, b.deleted_at
104			FROM   users a
105			JOIN   users b ON b.id > a.id
106			             AND LOWER(SUBSTR(b.username,1,4)) = LOWER(SUBSTR(a.username,1,4))
107			WHERE  a.deleted_at IS NULL
108			  AND  b.deleted_at IS NULL
109			ORDER  BY a.id
110			LIMIT  ?`
111	
112		rows, err := r.db.Query(q, limit)
113		if err != nil {
114			return nil, fmt.Errorf("repository: FindDuplicateUsers: %w", err)
115		}
116		defer rows.Close()
117	
118		var pairs []DuplicatePair
119		for rows.Next() {
120			var p DuplicatePair
121			a := &p.UserA
122			b := &p.UserB
123			if err := rows.Scan(
124				&a.ID, &a.Username, &a.Email, &a.PasswordHash, &a.Role,
125				&a.FailedAttempts, &a.LockedUntil, &a.CreatedAt, &a.UpdatedAt, &a.DeletedAt,
126				&b.ID, &b.Username, &b.Email, &b.PasswordHash, &b.Role,
127				&b.FailedAttempts, &b.LockedUntil, &b.CreatedAt, &b.UpdatedAt, &b.DeletedAt,
128			); err != nil {
129				return nil, fmt.Errorf("repository: FindDuplicateUsers: scan: %w", err)
130			}
131			// Simple similarity: prefix match gives at least 4 chars shared.
132			lenA := len(p.UserA.Username)
133			lenB := len(p.UserB.Username)
134			maxLen := lenA
135			if lenB > maxLen {
136				maxLen = lenB
137			}
138			diff := lenA - lenB
139			if diff < 0 {
140				diff = -diff
141			}
142			if maxLen > 0 {
143				p.Score = 1.0 - float64(diff)/float64(maxLen)
144			} else {
145				p.Score = 1.0
146			}
147			pairs = append(pairs, p)
148		}
149		return pairs, rows.Err()
150	}
151	
152	// MergeUsers merges duplicateID into primaryID:
153	//  1. Re-parents orders, ratings, comments, favorites_lists, notifications.
154	//  2. Inserts an entity_duplicates record.
155	//  3. Soft-deletes the duplicate user.
156	func (r *AdminRepository) MergeUsers(primaryID, duplicateID int64, mergedBy int64) error {
157		tx, err := r.db.Begin()
158		if err != nil {
159			return fmt.Errorf("repository: MergeUsers: begin tx: %w", err)
160		}
161		defer tx.Rollback() //nolint:errcheck
162	
163		// 1a. Re-parent orders.
164		if _, err := tx.Exec(`UPDATE orders SET user_id = ? WHERE user_id = ?`, primaryID, duplicateID); err != nil {
165			return fmt.Errorf("repository: MergeUsers: orders: %w", err)
166		}
167	
168		// 1b. Re-parent ratings (ignore conflicts — primary already has a rating).
169		if _, err := tx.Exec(`
170			UPDATE OR IGNORE ratings SET user_id = ? WHERE user_id = ?`,
171			primaryID, duplicateID); err != nil {
172			return fmt.Errorf("repository: MergeUsers: ratings: %w", err)
173		}
174	
175		// 1c. Re-parent comments.
176		if _, err := tx.Exec(`UPDATE comments SET user_id = ? WHERE user_id = ?`, primaryID, duplicateID); err != nil {
177			return fmt.Errorf("repository: MergeUsers: comments: %w", err)
178		}
179	
180		// 1d. Re-parent favorites_lists.
181		if _, err := tx.Exec(`UPDATE favorites_lists SET user_id = ? WHERE user_id = ?`, primaryID, duplicateID); err != nil {
182			return fmt.Errorf("repository: MergeUsers: favorites_lists: %w", err)
183		}
184	
185		// 1e. Re-parent notifications.
186		if _, err := tx.Exec(`UPDATE notifications SET user_id = ? WHERE user_id = ?`, primaryID, duplicateID); err != nil {
187			return fmt.Errorf("repository: MergeUsers: notifications: %w", err)
188		}
189	
190		// 2. Insert entity_duplicates record.
191		const insertDupQ = `
192			INSERT INTO entity_duplicates
193			            (entity_type, primary_id, duplicate_id, status, merged_by, merged_at)
194			VALUES      ('user', ?, ?, 'merged', ?, datetime('now'))`
195		if _, err := tx.Exec(insertDupQ, primaryID, duplicateID, mergedBy); err != nil {
196			return fmt.Errorf("repository: MergeUsers: insert entity_duplicates: %w", err)
197		}
198	
199		// 3. Soft-delete the duplicate.
200		const softDeleteQ = `
201			UPDATE users
202			SET    deleted_at = datetime('now'),
203			       updated_at = datetime('now')
204			WHERE  id = ? AND deleted_at IS NULL`
205		if _, err := tx.Exec(softDeleteQ, duplicateID); err != nil {
206			return fmt.Errorf("repository: MergeUsers: soft-delete duplicate: %w", err)
207		}
208	
209		return tx.Commit()
210	}

244	// WriteAuditLog inserts a row into the audit_log table.
245	func (r *AdminRepository) WriteAuditLog(actorID int64, action, entityType string, entityID int64, before, after interface{}, ip string) error {
246		var beforeJSON, afterJSON *string
247		if before != nil {
248			b, err := json.Marshal(before)
249			if err == nil {
250				s := string(b)
251				beforeJSON = &s
252			}
253		}
254		if after != nil {
255			b, err := json.Marshal(after)
256			if err == nil {
257				s := string(b)
258				afterJSON = &s
259			}
260		}
261		var ipPtr *string
262		if ip != "" {
263			ipPtr = &ip
264		}
265	
266		const q = `
267			INSERT INTO audit_log (actor_id, action, entity_type, entity_id, before_data, after_data, ip, created_at)
268			VALUES (?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, datetime('now'))`
269		_, err := r.db.Exec(q, actorID, action, entityType, entityID, beforeJSON, afterJSON, ipPtr)
270		if err != nil {
271			return fmt.Errorf("repository: WriteAuditLog: %w", err)
272		}
273		return nil
274	}

292			ORDER  BY created_at DESC

11	// AnalyticsRepository provides all KPI, spatial, and export queries.

16	// NewAnalyticsRepository returns an AnalyticsRepository backed by the given database.
17	func NewAnalyticsRepository(db *sql.DB) *AnalyticsRepository {
18		return &AnalyticsRepository{db: db}
19	}

21	// InventoryLevel summarises stock levels for one material.

24		Title        string
25		TotalQty     int

28	}

67	// ---------------------------------------------------------------
68	// KPI queries

71	// OrdersByStatus returns a map of order-status → count for all non-deleted orders.
72	func (r *AnalyticsRepository) OrdersByStatus() (map[string]int, error) {
73		const q = `
74			SELECT status, COUNT(*) AS cnt
75			FROM   orders
76			GROUP  BY status`
77	
78		rows, err := r.db.Query(q)
79		if err != nil {
80			return nil, fmt.Errorf("analytics: OrdersByStatus: %w", err)
81		}
82		defer rows.Close()
83	
84		out := make(map[string]int)
85		for rows.Next() {
86			var status string
87			var cnt int
88			if err := rows.Scan(&status, &cnt); err != nil {
89				return nil, fmt.Errorf("analytics: OrdersByStatus scan: %w", err)
90			}
91			out[status] = cnt
92		}
93		return out, rows.Err()
94	}
95	
96	// periodExpr converts a period string ("7d", "30d", "90d") to a SQLite
97	// datetime modifier.  Defaults to 30 days.
98	func periodExpr(period string) string {
99		switch period {
100		case "7d":
101			return "-7 days"
102		case "90d":
103			return "-90 days"
104		default:
105			return "-30 days"
106		}
107	}
108	
109	// FulfillmentRate returns the fraction of orders (in the given period) whose
110	// status is "completed", expressed as a value 0–100.
111	func (r *AnalyticsRepository) FulfillmentRate(period string) (float64, error) {
112		mod := periodExpr(period)
113		const q = `
114			SELECT
115				CAST(SUM(CASE WHEN status = 'completed' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS REAL) /
116				NULLIF(COUNT(*), 0) * 100
117			FROM orders
118			WHERE created_at >= datetime('now', ?)`
119	
120		var rate sql.NullFloat64
121		if err := r.db.QueryRow(q, mod).Scan(&rate); err != nil {
122			return 0, fmt.Errorf("analytics: FulfillmentRate: %w", err)
123		}
124		if !rate.Valid {
125			return 0, nil
126		}
127		return rate.Float64, nil
128	}
129	
130	// ReturnRate returns the fraction of completed orders (in the given period)
131	// that have at least one return request, expressed as a value 0–100.
132	func (r *AnalyticsRepository) ReturnRate(period string) (float64, error) {
133		mod := periodExpr(period)
134		const q = `
135			SELECT
136				CAST(COUNT(DISTINCT rr.order_id) AS REAL) /
137				NULLIF(COUNT(DISTINCT o.id), 0) * 100
138			FROM   orders o
139			LEFT   JOIN return_requests rr ON rr.order_id = o.id
140			WHERE  o.status = 'completed'
141			  AND  o.created_at >= datetime('now', ?)`
142	
143		var rate sql.NullFloat64
144		if err := r.db.QueryRow(q, mod).Scan(&rate); err != nil {
145			return 0, fmt.Errorf("analytics: ReturnRate: %w", err)
146		}
147		if !rate.Valid {
148			return 0, nil
149		}
150		return rate.Float64, nil
151	}

153	// InventoryLevels returns stock levels for all non-deleted, active materials.
154	func (r *AnalyticsRepository) InventoryLevels() ([]InventoryLevel, error) {

156			SELECT id, title, total_qty, available_qty, reserved_qty
157			FROM   materials

161		rows, err := r.db.Query(q)

164		}
165		defer rows.Close()

193	// TopMaterials returns the top N materials by total order-item count, with
194	// their average star rating.
195	func (r *AnalyticsRepository) TopMaterials(limit int) ([]MaterialStat, error) {
196		const q = `
197			SELECT m.id,
198			       m.title,
199			       COUNT(oi.id)                                    AS order_count,
200			       COALESCE(AVG(CAST(rt.stars AS REAL)), 0.0)     AS avg_rating
201			FROM   materials m
202			JOIN   order_items oi ON oi.material_id = m.id
203			LEFT   JOIN ratings rt ON rt.material_id = m.id
204			WHERE  m.deleted_at IS NULL
205			GROUP  BY m.id, m.title
206			ORDER  BY order_count DESC
207			LIMIT  ?`
208	
209		rows, err := r.db.Query(q, limit)
210		if err != nil {
211			return nil, fmt.Errorf("analytics: TopMaterials: %w", err)
212		}
213		defer rows.Close()
214	
215		var out []MaterialStat
216		for rows.Next() {
217			var s MaterialStat
218			if err := rows.Scan(&s.MaterialID, &s.Title, &s.OrderCount, &s.AvgRating); err != nil {
219				return nil, fmt.Errorf("analytics: TopMaterials scan: %w", err)
220			}
221			out = append(out, s)
222		}
223		return out, rows.Err()
224	}

230	// SaveKPISnapshot inserts a new kpi_snapshots row.
231	func (r *AnalyticsRepository) SaveKPISnapshot(name, dimension string, value float64, period string) error {
232		const q = `
233			INSERT INTO kpi_snapshots (metric_name, dimension, value, period, computed_at)
234			VALUES (?, ?, ?, ?, datetime('now'))`
235	
236		var dimPtr *string
237		if dimension != "" {
238			dimPtr = &dimension
239		}
240		var periodPtr *string
241		if period != "" {
242			periodPtr = &period
243		}
244	
245		if _, err := r.db.Exec(q, name, dimPtr, value, periodPtr); err != nil {
246			return fmt.Errorf("analytics: SaveKPISnapshot: %w", err)
247		}
248		return nil
249	}

251	// GetKPIHistory returns the most-recent `limit` snapshots for the given metric
252	// and dimension, ordered newest-first.
253	func (r *AnalyticsRepository) GetKPIHistory(name, dimension string, limit int) ([]models.KPISnapshot, error) {
254		const q = `
255			SELECT id, metric_name, dimension, value, period, computed_at
256			FROM   kpi_snapshots
257			WHERE  metric_name = ?
258			  AND  (dimension = ? OR (dimension IS NULL AND ? = ''))
259			ORDER  BY computed_at DESC
260			LIMIT  ?`
261	
262		rows, err := r.db.Query(q, name, dimension, dimension, limit)
263		if err != nil {
264			return nil, fmt.Errorf("analytics: GetKPIHistory: %w", err)
265		}
266		defer rows.Close()
267	
268		var out []models.KPISnapshot
269		for rows.Next() {
270			var k models.KPISnapshot
271			if err := rows.Scan(&k.ID, &k.MetricName, &k.Dimension, &k.Value, &k.Period, &k.ComputedAt); err != nil {
272				return nil, fmt.Errorf("analytics: GetKPIHistory scan: %w", err)
273			}
274			out = append(out, k)
275		}
276		return out, rows.Err()
277	}

279	// ---------------------------------------------------------------
280	// Instructor: course order stats
281	// ---------------------------------------------------------------
282	
283	// CourseOrderStats returns per-subject/grade order aggregates for all orders
284	// whose items map to materials with the given instructor's course subjects.
285	// Since orders are user-scoped, this approximates course-level stats by
286	// joining materials on subject/grade_level filtered to orders placed by
287	// users in the instructor's reach (here: all orders, scoped by instructorID
288	// being the creator in a hypothetical course_plans table; for now we use
289	// the materials table directly and group by subject/grade).
290	func (r *AnalyticsRepository) CourseOrderStats(instructorID int64) ([]CourseOrderStat, error) {
291		const q = `
292			SELECT
293				COALESCE(m.subject, 'Unknown')     AS subject,
294				COALESCE(m.grade_level, 'Unknown') AS grade,
295				COUNT(oi.id)                        AS ordered,
296				SUM(CASE WHEN o.status IN ('pending_payment','pending_shipment') THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS pending,
297				SUM(CASE WHEN oi.fulfillment_status = 'fulfilled' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END)               AS fulfilled
298			FROM   order_items oi
299			JOIN   orders   o ON o.id = oi.order_id
300			JOIN   materials m ON m.id = oi.material_id
301			WHERE  m.deleted_at IS NULL
302			GROUP  BY m.subject, m.grade_level
303			ORDER  BY subject, grade`
304	
305		rows, err := r.db.Query(q)
306		if err != nil {
307			return nil, fmt.Errorf("analytics: CourseOrderStats: %w", err)
308		}
309		defer rows.Close()
310	
311		var out []CourseOrderStat
312		for rows.Next() {
313			var s CourseOrderStat
314			if err := rows.Scan(&s.Subject, &s.Grade, &s.Ordered, &s.Pending, &s.Fulfilled); err != nil {
315				return nil, fmt.Errorf("analytics: CourseOrderStats scan: %w", err)
316			}
317			out = append(out, s)
318		}
319		return out, rows.Err()
320	}

326	// GetLocations returns all locations of the given type.  Pass "" to return all.
327	func (r *AnalyticsRepository) GetLocations(locType string) ([]models.Location, error) {
328		q := `SELECT id, name, type, geom_wkt, lat, lng, properties, created_at FROM locations`
329		args := []interface{}{}
330		if locType != "" {
331			q += ` WHERE type = ?`
332			args = append(args, locType)
333		}
334		q += ` ORDER BY name`
335	
336		rows, err := r.db.Query(q, args...)
337		if err != nil {
338			return nil, fmt.Errorf("analytics: GetLocations: %w", err)
339		}
340		defer rows.Close()
341	
342		var out []models.Location
343		for rows.Next() {
344			var l models.Location
345			if err := rows.Scan(&l.ID, &l.Name, &l.Type, &l.GeomWKT, &l.Lat, &l.Lng, &l.Properties, &l.CreatedAt); err != nil {
346				return nil, fmt.Errorf("analytics: GetLocations scan: %w", err)
347			}
348			out = append(out, l)
349		}
350		return out, rows.Err()
351	}

353	// GetSpatialAggregates returns all spatial_aggregates rows for the given layer type.
354	func (r *AnalyticsRepository) GetSpatialAggregates(layerType string) ([]models.SpatialAggregate, error) {
355		const q = `
356			SELECT id, layer_type, cell_key, metric, value, computed_at
357			FROM   spatial_aggregates
358			WHERE  layer_type = ?
359			ORDER  BY cell_key`
360	
361		rows, err := r.db.Query(q, layerType)
362		if err != nil {
363			return nil, fmt.Errorf("analytics: GetSpatialAggregates: %w", err)
364		}
365		defer rows.Close()
366	
367		var out []models.SpatialAggregate
368		for rows.Next() {
369			var sa models.SpatialAggregate
370			if err := rows.Scan(&sa.ID, &sa.LayerType, &sa.CellKey, &sa.Metric, &sa.Value, &sa.ComputedAt); err != nil {
371				return nil, fmt.Errorf("analytics: GetSpatialAggregates scan: %w", err)
372			}
373			out = append(out, sa)
374		}
375		return out, rows.Err()
376	}
377	
378	// UpsertSpatialAggregate inserts or replaces a spatial_aggregates row.
379	func (r *AnalyticsRepository) UpsertSpatialAggregate(layerType, cellKey, metric string, value float64) error {
380		const q = `
381			INSERT INTO spatial_aggregates (layer_type, cell_key, metric, value, computed_at)
382			VALUES (?, ?, ?, ?, datetime('now'))
383			ON CONFLICT(layer_type, cell_key, metric)
384			DO UPDATE SET value = excluded.value, computed_at = excluded.computed_at`
385	
386		if _, err := r.db.Exec(q, layerType, cellKey, metric, value); err != nil {
387			return fmt.Errorf("analytics: UpsertSpatialAggregate: %w", err)
388		}
389		return nil
390	}
391	
392	// ComputeGridAggregation groups locations of the given layerType into lat/lng
393	// grid cells of size gridSizeKm (approximated as degrees: 1 deg ≈ 111 km),
394	// counts the locations per cell, and saves the result to spatial_aggregates.
395	func (r *AnalyticsRepository) ComputeGridAggregation(layerType, metric string, gridSizeKm float64) error {
396		locs, err := r.GetLocations(layerType)
397		if err != nil {
398			return fmt.Errorf("analytics: ComputeGridAggregation: fetch locations: %w", err)
399		}
400	
401		const degPerKm = 1.0 / 111.0
402		gridStep := gridSizeKm * degPerKm
403	
404		counts := make(map[string]float64)
405		for _, loc := range locs {
406			if loc.Lat == nil || loc.Lng == nil {
407				continue
408			}
409			cellLat := math.Floor(*loc.Lat/gridStep) * gridStep
410			cellLng := math.Floor(*loc.Lng/gridStep) * gridStep
411			cellKey := fmt.Sprintf("%.6f,%.6f", cellLat, cellLng)
412			counts[cellKey]++
413		}
414	
415		for cellKey, count := range counts {
416			if err := r.UpsertSpatialAggregate(layerType, cellKey, metric, count); err != nil {
417				return fmt.Errorf("analytics: ComputeGridAggregation: upsert %q: %w", cellKey, err)
418			}
419		}
420		return nil
421	}
422	
423	// ---------------------------------------------------------------
424	// Exports
425	// ---------------------------------------------------------------
426	
427	// ExportOrders returns a flat slice of order rows for CSV export.  All
428	// filter parameters are optional (pass "" to skip).
429	func (r *AnalyticsRepository) ExportOrders(status, dateFrom, dateTo string) ([]OrderExportRow, error) {
430		where := "1=1"
431		args := []interface{}{}
432	
433		if status != "" {
434			where += " AND o.status = ?"
435			args = append(args, status)
436		}
437		if dateFrom != "" {
438			where += " AND o.created_at >= ?"
439			args = append(args, dateFrom)
440		}
441		if dateTo != "" {
442			where += " AND o.created_at <= ?"
443			args = append(args, dateTo)
444		}
445	
446		q := `
447			SELECT
448				o.id,
449				u.username,
450				u.email,
451				o.status,
452				o.total_amount,
453				o.created_at,
454				COUNT(oi.id) AS item_count
455			FROM   orders o
456			JOIN   users u  ON u.id = o.user_id
457			LEFT   JOIN order_items oi ON oi.order_id = o.id
458			WHERE  ` + where + `
459			GROUP  BY o.id, u.username, u.email, o.status, o.total_amount, o.created_at
460			ORDER  BY o.created_at DESC`
461	
462		rows, err := r.db.Query(q, args...)
463		if err != nil {
464			return nil, fmt.Errorf("analytics: ExportOrders: %w", err)
465		}
466		defer rows.Close()
467	
468		var out []OrderExportRow
469		for rows.Next() {
470			var row OrderExportRow
471			if err := rows.Scan(
472				&row.OrderID, &row.UserName, &row.UserEmail,
473				&row.Status, &row.TotalAmount, &row.CreatedAt, &row.ItemCount,
474			); err != nil {
475				return nil, fmt.Errorf("analytics: ExportOrders scan: %w", err)
476			}
477			out = append(out, row)
478		}
479		return out, rows.Err()
480	}
481	
482	// ExportDistribution returns a flat slice of distribution-event rows for CSV
483	// export, optionally filtered by date range.
484	func (r *AnalyticsRepository) ExportDistribution(dateFrom, dateTo string) ([]DistribExportRow, error) {
485		where := "1=1"
486		args := []interface{}{}
487	
488		if dateFrom != "" {
489			where += " AND de.occurred_at >= ?"
490			args = append(args, dateFrom)
491		}
492		if dateTo != "" {
493			where += " AND de.occurred_at <= ?"
494			args = append(args, dateTo)
495		}
496	
497		q := `
498			SELECT
499				COALESCE(de.scan_id, ''),
500				de.event_type,
501				m.title,
502				COALESCE(u.username, ''),
503				de.occurred_at
504			FROM   distribution_events de
505			JOIN   materials m ON m.id = de.material_id
506			LEFT   JOIN users u ON u.id = de.actor_id
507			WHERE  ` + where + `
508			ORDER  BY de.occurred_at DESC`
509	
510		rows, err := r.db.Query(q, args...)
511		if err != nil {
512			return nil, fmt.Errorf("analytics: ExportDistribution: %w", err)
513		}
514		defer rows.Close()
515	
516		var out []DistribExportRow
517		for rows.Next() {
518			var row DistribExportRow
519			if err := rows.Scan(
520				&row.ScanID, &row.EventType, &row.MaterialTitle,
521				&row.ActorName, &row.OccurredAt,
522			); err != nil {
523				return nil, fmt.Errorf("analytics: ExportDistribution scan: %w", err)
524			}

531	// Dashboard stat counts (used by GET /api/stats/:stat)
532	// ---------------------------------------------------------------
533	
534	// CountOrdersForUser returns the number of orders placed by a specific user.
535	func (r *AnalyticsRepository) CountOrdersForUser(userID int64) (int, error) {
536		var n int
537		err := r.db.QueryRow(`SELECT COUNT(*) FROM orders WHERE user_id = ?`, userID).Scan(&n)
538		return n, err

540	
541	// CountFavoritesListsForUser returns the number of favorites lists owned by a user.
542	func (r *AnalyticsRepository) CountFavoritesListsForUser(userID int64) (int, error) {
543		var n int
544		err := r.db.QueryRow(`SELECT COUNT(*) FROM favorites_lists WHERE user_id = ?`, userID).Scan(&n)
545		return n, err

547	
548	// CountRecentMaterialViewsForUser returns the number of distinct materials
549	// viewed by the user in the last 30 days.

553			SELECT COUNT(DISTINCT material_id)
554			FROM   browse_history
555			WHERE  user_id = ?
556			  AND  viewed_at >= datetime('now', '-30 days')`,
557			userID,
558		).Scan(&n)
559		return n, err
560	}

576	// ResolveReturn closes a return request as approved or rejected.
577	func (r *OrderRepository) ResolveReturn(id int64, resolvedBy int64, approved bool) error {
578		status := "rejected"
579		if approved {
580			status = "approved"
581		}
582	
583		const q = `
584			UPDATE return_requests
585			SET    status      = ?,
586			       resolved_at = datetime('now'),
587			       resolved_by = ?
588			WHERE  id = ? AND status = 'pending'`
589	
590		res, err := r.db.Exec(q, status, resolvedBy, id)
591		if err != nil {
592			return fmt.Errorf("repository: ResolveReturn: %w", err)
593		}
594		n, err := res.RowsAffected()
595		if err != nil {
596			return err
597		}
598		if n == 0 {
599			return fmt.Errorf("repository: ResolveReturn: return request %d not found or already resolved", id)
600		}
601		return nil
602	}

{"file_path": "/Users/rollin/eaglepoint/w2t86/repo/migrations/003_financial_transactions.sql", "content": "-- 003_financial_transactions.sql
-- Adds the financial_transactions table for return/refund/charge traceability.

CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS financial_transactions (
    id                INTEGER PRIMARY KEY,
    order_id          INTEGER REFERENCES orders(id),
    return_request_id INTEGER REFERENCES return_requests(id),
    type              TEXT    NOT NULL,  -- 'refund' | 'charge' | 'adjustment'
    amount            REAL    NOT NULL DEFAULT 0,
    status            TEXT    DEFAULT 'pending',  -- 'pending' | 'completed' | 'failed'
    reference         TEXT,
    note              TEXT,
    actor_id          INTEGER REFERENCES users(id),
    created_at        TEXT    DEFAULT (datetime('now')),
    updated_at        TEXT    DEFAULT (datetime('now'))
);

CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_financial_txn_order  ON financial_transactions(order_id);
CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_financial_txn_return ON financial_transactions(return_request_id);
"}

108			FROM   users a
109			JOIN   users b ON b.id > a.id

Updated task #27 status

**Step C done.** Complete rewrite of `FindDuplicateUsers`:
- **Pass 1**: SQL self-join on `a.email = b.email` → score 1.0 (definite duplicates, no Go processing needed)
- **Pass 2**: SQL self-join on `a.date_of_birth = b.date_of_birth AND a.email != b.email` → for each candidate pair, computes Levenshtein edit distance between lowercased usernames in Go; similarity = `1 - dist/maxLen`; pairs with similarity ≥ 0.6 are included
- `levenshtein()` uses the standard two-row DP algorithm (O(m×n) time, O(n) space)
- Results sorted by score descending, capped at `limit`

{"file_path": "/Users/rollin/eaglepoint/w2t86/repo/web/static/tiles/README.md", "content": "# Offline Map Tiles

The geospatial map (`/analytics/map`) uses Leaflet.js with locally-served XYZ
tiles at `/static/tiles/{z}/{x}/{y}.png`.  This directory is a placeholder —
tile files must be downloaded separately and placed here before the map will
render properly.

## Tile URL pattern

```
web/static/tiles/{z}/{x}/{y}.png
```

`z` = zoom level (0–18), `x`/`y` = tile column/row at that zoom.

## Option 1 — Download with the bundled script (recommended)

A helper script is provided at `scripts/download_tiles.sh`.  It fetches a
bounding-box tile set from OpenStreetMap's tile CDN at zoom levels 0–12,
which covers a region at a manageable file size (~50–200 MB depending on area).

```bash
# Adjust the bounding box for your district's region
bash scripts/download_tiles.sh \
  --min-lon -97.0 --max-lon -96.0 \
  --min-lat  32.5 --max-lat  33.5 \
  --min-zoom 0    --max-zoom 12
```

> **Usage policy**: OpenStreetMap tile CDN is for low-volume, non-production
> use only.  For production deployments, self-host tiles using an MBTiles
> bundle or a tile server such as [tileserver-gl](https://github.com/maptiler/tileserver-gl).

## Option 2 — MBTiles bundle (production)

1. Download a regional MBTiles file from [OpenMapTiles](https://openmaptiles.org/)
   or generate one with [Planetiler](https://github.com/onthegomap/planetiler).
2. Serve it with **tileserver-gl** or **Martin**.
3. Update `web/static/js/map.js` line 4 to point at the tile server URL
   instead of `/static/tiles/{z}/{x}/{y}.png`.

## Option 3 — Quick dev tile fetch (single command)

If you have `wget` installed, run:

```bash
bash scripts/download_tiles.sh --quick
```

This fetches a small default region (zoom 0–6 world overview, ~5 MB) sufficient
for development and demos.

## Directory structure after hydration

```
web/static/tiles/
  0/0/0.png
  1/0/0.png  1/0/1.png  1/1/0.png  1/1/1.png
  2/...
  ...
```

## Notes

- The map will silently skip any missing tile (see `errorTileUrl: ''` in `map.js`)
  so the application will run without tiles — the basemap simply appears blank.
- Tile files are excluded from version control via `.gitignore`
  (`web/static/tiles/**/*.png`).
"}

{"file_path": "/Users/rollin/eaglepoint/w2t86/repo/scripts/download_tiles.sh", "content": "#!/usr/bin/env bash
# download_tiles.sh — fetch XYZ PNG tiles from OpenStreetMap into web/static/tiles/
#
# Usage:
#   bash scripts/download_tiles.sh [OPTIONS]
#
# Options:
#   --min-lon <float>   Western longitude  (default: -180)
#   --max-lon <float>   Eastern longitude  (default:  180)
#   --min-lat <float>   Southern latitude  (default:  -85)
#   --max-lat <float>   Northern latitude  (default:   85)
#   --min-zoom <int>    Minimum zoom level (default:    0)
#   --max-zoom <int>    Maximum zoom level (default:    8)
#   --quick             Fetch world overview only (zoom 0-6, ~5 MB)
#
# Requirements: bash, curl, bc, awk
#
# Tile server: https://tile.openstreetmap.org/{z}/{x}/{y}.png
# Rate limit: 1 request/second (OSM tile usage policy).
# For production use a self-hosted tile server — see tiles/README.md.

set -euo pipefail

TILE_DIR="$(dirname "$0")/../web/static/tiles"
TILE_URL="https://tile.openstreetmap.org"
DELAY=1  # seconds between requests (OSM policy)

# Defaults: whole world
MIN_LON=-180
MAX_LON=180
MIN_LAT=-85
MAX_LAT=85
MIN_ZOOM=0
MAX_ZOOM=8

# Parse arguments
while [[ $# -gt 0 ]]; do
  case "$1" in
    --quick)    MIN_LON=-180; MAX_LON=180; MIN_LAT=-85; MAX_LAT=85; MIN_ZOOM=0; MAX_ZOOM=6; shift ;;
    --min-lon)  MIN_LON="$2"; shift 2 ;;
    --max-lon)  MAX_LON="$2"; shift 2 ;;
    --min-lat)  MIN_LAT="$2"; shift 2 ;;
    --max-lat)  MAX_LAT="$2"; shift 2 ;;
    --min-zoom) MIN_ZOOM="$2"; shift 2 ;;
    --max-zoom) MAX_ZOOM="$2"; shift 2 ;;
    *) echo "Unknown option: $1" >&2; exit 1 ;;
  esac
done

# Convert lon/lat to tile x/y at a given zoom level.
# Uses awk for floating-point arithmetic.
lon_to_x() {
  local lon=$1 zoom=$2
  awk -v lon="$lon" -v z="$zoom" 'BEGIN {
    n = 2^z
    x = int((lon + 180) / 360 * n)
    print x
  }'
}

lat_to_y() {
  local lat=$1 zoom=$2
  awk -v lat="$lat" -v z="$zoom" 'BEGIN {
    pi = 3.14159265358979
    lat_r = lat * pi / 180
    n = 2^z
    y = int((1 - log(sin(lat_r) + 1/cos(lat_r)) / pi) / 2 * n)
    print y
  }'
}

mkdir -p "$TILE_DIR"

echo "Downloading tiles zoom=${MIN_ZOOM}-${MAX_ZOOM} into ${TILE_DIR}"
echo "Bounds: lon [${MIN_LON}, ${MAX_LON}]  lat [${MIN_LAT}, ${MAX_LAT}]"
echo "Rate-limited to 1 tile/second (OSM policy)."
echo ""

TOTAL=0
SKIP=0

for (( z=MIN_ZOOM; z<=MAX_ZOOM; z++ )); do
  x_min=$(lon_to_x "$MIN_LON" "$z")
  x_max=$(lon_to_x "$MAX_LON" "$z")
  y_min=$(lat_to_y "$MAX_LAT" "$z")   # note: lat_to_y is inverted (north = lower y)
  y_max=$(lat_to_y "$MIN_LAT" "$z")
  max_tile=$(( (1 << z) - 1 ))

  # Clamp to valid range
  [[ $x_min -lt 0 ]] && x_min=0
  [[ $y_min -lt 0 ]] && y_min=0
  [[ $x_max -gt $max_tile ]] && x_max=$max_tile
  [[ $y_max -gt $max_tile ]] && y_max=$max_tile

  for (( x=x_min; x<=x_max; x++ )); do
    for (( y=y_min; y<=y_max; y++ )); do
      dest="${TILE_DIR}/${z}/${x}/${y}.png"
      if [[ -f "$dest" ]]; then
        (( SKIP++ )) || true
        continue
      fi
      mkdir -p "$(dirname "$dest")"
      url="${TILE_URL}/${z}/${x}/${y}.png"
      if curl -sSf -o "$dest" \
              -H "User-Agent: DistrictPortal-TileFetcher/1.0 (on-prem)" \
              "$url"; then
        (( TOTAL++ )) || true
        printf "  z=%d x=%d y=%d  [%d fetched, %d skipped]\r" "$z" "$x" "$y" "$TOTAL" "$SKIP"
      else
        echo "  WARN: failed to fetch ${url}" >&2
        rm -f "$dest"
      fi
      sleep "$DELAY"
    done
  done
done

echo ""
echo "Done. ${TOTAL} tiles downloaded, ${SKIP} already present."
"}

86	// DuplicatePair represents two users that may be the same person.
87	type DuplicatePair struct {
88		UserA models.User
89		UserB models.User
90		Score float64 // similarity 0-1
91	}

93	// FindDuplicateUsers returns user pairs that are likely the same person.
94	// Two detection strategies are applied:
95	//  1. Exact email match → score 1.0 (definite duplicate).
96	//  2. Same date_of_birth (non-null) + Levenshtein username similarity ≥ 0.6
97	//     → score equal to the computed similarity (fuzzy match).
98	//
99	// Results are sorted by descending score, capped at limit.
100	// Soft-deleted users are excluded.
101	func (r *AdminRepository) FindDuplicateUsers(limit int) ([]DuplicatePair, error) {
102		// ---- Pass 1: exact email duplicates (score 1.0) ----
103		const exactQ = `
104			SELECT a.id, a.username, a.email, a.password_hash, a.role,
105			       a.failed_attempts, a.locked_until, a.date_of_birth,
106			       a.created_at, a.updated_at, a.deleted_at,
107			       b.id, b.username, b.email, b.password_hash, b.role,
108			       b.failed_attempts, b.locked_until, b.date_of_birth,
109			       b.created_at, b.updated_at, b.deleted_at
110			FROM   users a
111			JOIN   users b ON b.id > a.id AND a.email = b.email
112			WHERE  a.deleted_at IS NULL AND b.deleted_at IS NULL
113			ORDER  BY a.id`
114	
115		exactRows, err := r.db.Query(exactQ)
116		if err != nil {
117			return nil, fmt.Errorf("repository: FindDuplicateUsers (exact): %w", err)
118		}
119		pairs, err := scanUserPairs(exactRows)
120		if err != nil {
121			return nil, fmt.Errorf("repository: FindDuplicateUsers (exact scan): %w", err)
122		}
123		for i := range pairs {
124			pairs[i].Score = 1.0
125		}
126	
127		// ---- Pass 2: same DOB candidates — fuzzy username match via Levenshtein ----
128		const dobQ = `
129			SELECT a.id, a.username, a.email, a.password_hash, a.role,
130			       a.failed_attempts, a.locked_until, a.date_of_birth,
131			       a.created_at, a.updated_at, a.deleted_at,
132			       b.id, b.username, b.email, b.password_hash, b.role,
133			       b.failed_attempts, b.locked_until, b.date_of_birth,
134			       b.created_at, b.updated_at, b.deleted_at
135			FROM   users a
136			JOIN   users b ON b.id > a.id
137			             AND a.date_of_birth IS NOT NULL
138			             AND a.date_of_birth = b.date_of_birth
139			             AND a.email != b.email
140			WHERE  a.deleted_at IS NULL AND b.deleted_at IS NULL
141			ORDER  BY a.id`
142	
143		dobRows, err := r.db.Query(dobQ)
144		if err != nil {
145			return nil, fmt.Errorf("repository: FindDuplicateUsers (dob): %w", err)
146		}
147		dobCandidates, err := scanUserPairs(dobRows)
148		if err != nil {
149			return nil, fmt.Errorf("repository: FindDuplicateUsers (dob scan): %w", err)
150		}
151	
152		// Compute Levenshtein similarity for each same-DOB pair; keep if score ≥ 0.6.
153		const fuzzyThreshold = 0.6
154		for _, p := range dobCandidates {
155			score := usernameSimilarity(p.UserA.Username, p.UserB.Username)
156			if score >= fuzzyThreshold {
157				p.Score = score
158				pairs = append(pairs, p)
159			}
160		}
161	
162		// Sort by score descending, apply limit.
163		sortDuplicatePairs(pairs)
164		if limit > 0 && len(pairs) > limit {
165			pairs = pairs[:limit]
166		}
167		return pairs, nil
168	}

174	// scanUserPairs scans rows produced by a self-join query that returns two
175	// consecutive user column sets (without a score column).
176	func scanUserPairs(rows *sql.Rows) ([]DuplicatePair, error) {
177		defer rows.Close()
178		var pairs []DuplicatePair
179		for rows.Next() {
180			var p DuplicatePair
181			a, b := &p.UserA, &p.UserB
182			if err := rows.Scan(
183				&a.ID, &a.Username, &a.Email, &a.PasswordHash, &a.Role,
184				&a.FailedAttempts, &a.LockedUntil, &a.DateOfBirth,
185				&a.CreatedAt, &a.UpdatedAt, &a.DeletedAt,
186				&b.ID, &b.Username, &b.Email, &b.PasswordHash, &b.Role,
187				&b.FailedAttempts, &b.LockedUntil, &b.DateOfBirth,
188				&b.CreatedAt, &b.UpdatedAt, &b.DeletedAt,
189			); err != nil {
190				return nil, err
191			}
192			pairs = append(pairs, p)
193		}
194		return pairs, rows.Err()
195	}

197	// usernameSimilarity returns a 0–1 Levenshtein-based similarity score for two
198	// usernames (case-insensitive).
199	func usernameSimilarity(a, b string) float64 {
200		a = strings.ToLower(a)
201		b = strings.ToLower(b)
202		maxLen := len(a)
203		if len(b) > maxLen {
204			maxLen = len(b)
205		}
206		if maxLen == 0 {
207			return 1.0
208		}
209		dist := levenshtein(a, b)
210		return 1.0 - float64(dist)/float64(maxLen)
211	}
212	
213	// levenshtein computes the edit distance between two strings using the
214	// standard dynamic-programming approach.
215	func levenshtein(a, b string) int {
216		ra, rb := []rune(a), []rune(b)
217		la, lb := len(ra), len(rb)
218		if la == 0 {
219			return lb
220		}
221		if lb == 0 {
222			return la
223		}
224		// Use two rows of the DP table to save memory.
225		prev := make([]int, lb+1)
226		curr := make([]int, lb+1)
227		for j := 0; j <= lb; j++ {
228			prev[j] = j
229		}
230		for i := 1; i <= la; i++ {
231			curr[0] = i
232			for j := 1; j <= lb; j++ {
233				cost := 1
234				if ra[i-1] == rb[j-1] {
235					cost = 0
236				}
237				curr[j] = minInt(
238					curr[j-1]+1,        // insertion
239					prev[j]+1,          // deletion
240					prev[j-1]+cost,     // substitution
241				)
242			}
243			prev, curr = curr, prev

**Step A done.** The key change: Pass 2 no longer uses DOB as a mandatory SQL join key. Instead:

- SQL pre-filter uses `OR` logic — candidates have either same DOB **or** same 4-char username prefix (broadens the candidate set without hard-coding which must match)
- Go computes `score = 0.6 × username_similarity + 0.4 × dob_match` for every candidate
- Threshold is 0.65 — deliberately above what either signal can achieve alone (DOB-only max = 0.4, username-only max = 0.6), so a pair must have **both** a similar name and a matching DOB to qualify
- Exact email match remains a separate pass at score 1.0

3	// Integration tests for the w2t86 portal.
4	//
5	// Template rendering notes:
6	//   - The Fiber app is built WITH the html template engine pointing to
7	//     ../../web/templates (relative to this package).  Most handlers call
8	//     c.Render, so we need the engine wired up.
9	//   - For handlers that are templated but also have HTMX paths that return
10	//     plain text/JSON, we send HX-Request: true to get text responses.
11	//   - For handlers whose success path is a redirect (302/303), we test just
12	//     the redirect status code — no template is rendered.
13	//   - For handlers that render a full-page template on success, we accept any
14	//     2xx status (the template engine will return 200 if the template exists,
15	//     or 500 if it does not; add a comment explaining which outcome is expected).
16	//
17	// IMPORTANT: Fiber v2 with multiple app.Group("", mw...) shares middleware
18	// across ALL requests because the empty prefix makes the group middleware global.
19	// To avoid cross-group RBAC interference, we use per-route middleware by
20	// passing the RBAC handler directly as an additional argument to each route
21	// registration instead of using groups with empty prefix for RBAC.

139		engine.AddFunc("dict", func(values ...interface{}) (map[string]interface{}, error) {

143			dict := make(map[string]interface{}, len(values)/2)
144			for i := 0; i < len(values); i += 2 {

335	// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
336	// loginAs — creates a user with the given role, logs in, returns cookie string.
337	// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
338	
339	// loginAs creates a user with the given role, sends POST /login, and returns the
340	// "session_token=<value>" cookie string suitable for use in subsequent requests.
341	// The returned string can be passed directly as the Cookie header value.
342	func loginAs(t *testing.T, app *fiber.App, db *sql.DB, role string) string {
343		t.Helper()
344	
345		// Create a unique username per call so parallel tests don't collide.
346		username := fmt.Sprintf("testuser_%s_%d", role, time.Now().UnixNano())
347		password := "TestPassword123!"
348		email := username + "@example.com"
349	
350		userRepo := repository.NewUserRepository(db)
351		sessionRepo := repository.NewSessionRepository(db)
352		cfg := &config.Config{SessionSecret: "test-secret"}
353		authSvc := services.NewAuthService(userRepo, sessionRepo, cfg)
354	
355		if _, err := authSvc.Register(username, email, password, role); err != nil {
356			t.Fatalf("loginAs: register user: %v", err)
357		}
358	
359		body := fmt.Sprintf("username=%s&password=%s", username, password)
360		req := httptest.NewRequest(http.MethodPost, "/login", strings.NewReader(body))
361		req.Header.Set("Content-Type", "application/x-www-form-urlencoded")
362	
363		resp, err := app.Test(req, -1)
364		if err != nil {
365			t.Fatalf("loginAs: app.Test: %v", err)
366		}
367		defer resp.Body.Close()
368	
369		// Login success redirects to /dashboard (302).
370		if resp.StatusCode != http.StatusFound && resp.StatusCode != http.StatusOK {
371			b, _ := io.ReadAll(resp.Body)
372			t.Fatalf("loginAs: unexpected status %d; body: %s", resp.StatusCode, string(b))
373		}
374	
375		// Extract the Set-Cookie header.
376		for _, c := range resp.Cookies() {
377			if c.Name == "session_token" {
378				return "session_token=" + c.Value
379			}
380		}
381		t.Fatal("loginAs: no session_token cookie found in response")
382		return ""
383	}

385	// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
386	// makeRequest — fires a request against the test Fiber app.
387	// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
388	
389	// makeRequest builds a request and calls app.Test.
390	// - method: HTTP verb ("GET", "POST", etc.)
391	// - path: request path ("/orders/1")
392	// - body: raw request body (empty string for no body)
393	// - cookie: cookie header value (e.g. "session_token=abc123"); empty for none
394	// - contentType: e.g. "application/x-www-form-urlencoded"; empty defaults to no Content-Type
395	func makeRequest(app *fiber.App, method, path, body, cookie, contentType string, extraHeaders ...map[string]string) *http.Response {
396		var bodyReader io.Reader
397		if body != "" {
398			bodyReader = strings.NewReader(body)
399		}
400		req := httptest.NewRequest(method, path, bodyReader)
401		if cookie != "" {
402			req.Header.Set("Cookie", cookie)
403		}
404		if contentType != "" {
405			req.Header.Set("Content-Type", contentType)
406		}
407		for _, h := range extraHeaders {
408			for k, v := range h {
409				req.Header.Set(k, v)
410			}
411		}
412		resp, err := app.Test(req, -1)
413		if err != nil {
414			// Return a synthetic 500 response on internal test errors.
415			return &http.Response{StatusCode: http.StatusInternalServerError}
416		}
417		return resp
418	}
419	
420	// htmxHeaders returns a map with HX-Request: true for use with makeRequest's
421	// extraHeaders parameter.
422	func htmxHeaders() map[string]string {
423		return map[string]string{"HX-Request": "true"}
424	}

427	// createTestMaterial — inserts a test material directly into the DB.
428	// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
429	
430	// createTestMaterial inserts an active material row directly into the DB and
431	// returns the populated model.
432	func createTestMaterial(t *testing.T, db *sql.DB) *models.Material {
433		t.Helper()
434		repo := repository.NewMaterialRepository(db)
435		title := fmt.Sprintf("Test Book %d", time.Now().UnixNano())
436		m := &models.Material{
437			Title:        title,
438			TotalQty:     10,
439			AvailableQty: 10,
440			ReservedQty:  0,
441			Status:       "active",
442		}
443		created, err := repo.Create(m)
